name: tonality
definition: Narrow, stable spectral peaks (a harmonic comb) versus broadband noise; the primary discriminator between idle-tonal engine content and moving-rough broadband road content in the sub-bass band.
indicators: narrow stable spectral peaks vs broadband
engine firing fundamental and low harmonics live below 80 Hz

detection.band: sub-bass 20-80 Hz (first-class; adaptive window per band)
detection.measure: HNR = sum(band-bin power where power > localMedian x PEAK_K) / sum(band-bin power), clamped [0,1]; PEAK_K=3
detection.provenance: sub-bass discovery, 2026-07-04 stop-vs-drive test; lib/squelch.js computeSpectralChaos

confidence.basis: peak margin (spectral peak prominence over local median); reliability capped 0 on clipping, near-floor, or speech contamination

notes: Adjacent 80-250 Hz 'low' band does NOT discriminate (0.72 vs 0.75, broadband either way) - this is why sub-bass is first-class. Near-silence (within BAND_SNR_MIN=6dB of the per-run noise floor) emits confidence 0 rather than a spurious high reading.
